時々吹雪も起きるし、視界が悪すぎるわね……: Blizzards keep popping up too, and the visibility is terrible...
でもアーデルハイトさんの作ってくれた装備すごいですね～。<br>インナーの防寒着:
But Adelheid's gear is amazing~.
The insulating layer is so light, and the boots grip the snow~.
モンスターの襲撃と並んで最も恐ろしいのは、転倒からの滑落だ。<br>決して油断せ:
As dangerous as monster attacks is slipping and falling.
If you tumble off a cliff, it's over regardless of the enemy.
そんな歩き方、かっこ悪いしダサダサでしょ～？<br>って、あぶなっ！ もうちょっ:
Walking like that looks so uncool and lame though~?
——Whoa! I almost fell right there!
気をつけなさいよ。<br>司令官の言う通り、油断大敵よ。:
Be careful.
Just like the Commander said, never let your guard down.
あれ～？ あっれ～？ あれあれあれ～？<br>ソフィアってば、あんなにおにーさん:
Hmmm~? Hey~? What what what~?
Sophia, you were always butting heads with Onii-san so much~.
おにーさんに随分素直に従ってるじゃ～ん？<br>どうしちゃったのぉ？ どういう心:
Now you're being real obedient toward him, aren't you~?
What happened? Had a change of heart?
うるさいわね！<br>べ、別にこの男に従ってるわけじゃないわ！:
Mind your own business!
I-I'm not being obedient to this man!
偶然……そう、偶然、私も、同じ、作戦を思いついていただけ！:
Coincidence... yes, coincidence! I just happened to think
of the same strategy, that's all!
嘘くさぁ～。<br>……あと、顔まっかだょ？:
Suuure~.
...Also, your face is bright red?
な、違うわよ！<br>この階層が寒いからよ！ 見なさいよ、マリナだって、真っ赤に:
N-no it's not!
It's the cold on this layer! Look, even Marina is turning bri——
いやあ、さっきから周辺にチラチラと見えてる鉱石が気になって気になって。<br>け:
Actually, the ore I keep catching glimpses of is driving me crazy~.
But we don't have time to mine at our leisure......
そうね。厄災の撃退が優先事項だもの。: Right. Defeating the Calamity is the priority.
そうなんですよ……ですから、こうして、指をくわえて、<br>鉱石拾いをぐっと……:
That's right... so I'm biting my tongue and holding myself back,
really restraining myself from scooping up the ore...
我慢して我慢して……我慢しすぎて、顔色が悪くなってきたんですよ～。:
Holding back, holding back......
I held back so much my color's starting to drain~.
今は厄災撃退が最優先！<br>鉱石はその後でいくらでも採掘すればいいじゃないの！:
Defeating the Calamity is the top priority right now!
You can mine all the ore you want once that's done!
じゃあ、先を急ぐわよ。みんな足元は慎重に……: Let's push ahead then. Everyone, watch your step...
ここから先は積雪が激しい地域みたいですね～。<br>今までの凍った地面とは毛色が:
Looks like we're entering a region with heavy snowfall from here~.
This is a different beast from the frozen ground up until now.
今までと雪質も違うようだな。<br>パウダースノーってやつか。:
The snow texture seems different too.
Is this what they call powder snow?
雪の上ならスキーとかで滑る方が早いんじゃないの～？: In snow like this, wouldn't it be faster to slide along on skis~?
ベリサちゃん、スキーなんてできるの？<br>かっこいいですねぇ～。:
Verisa, do you know how to ski?
How cool~.
えっ？ やったことはないよ～。: Huh? I've never actually tried it~.
ほら、馬鹿なこと言ってないで先を急ぐわよ。: Come on, quit talking nonsense and let's push ahead.
雪庇を踏み抜き、滑落しそうになるソフィアの腕を、<br>咄嗟に<user>が掴ん:
Sophia broke through a snow cornice and nearly plunged off the
edge— %user% grabbed her arm on pure instinct.
こっわ～。まるで落とし穴じゃない！: That was terrifying~. It was like a hidden pitfall!
し、死ぬかと……思った……: I-I thought I was going to... die...
雪庇だ。一見、地面に見えてもその下は奈落だぞ。<br>誰も踏み込んだことのない雪:
That's a snow cornice. It may look like ground but below is a chasm.
In untrodden snowfields, always watch your footing. Got it?
アーデルハイトから渡されたスティックがあっただろ？<br>ここから先は足元を確認:
You all still have that stick Adelheid gave us, right?
From here on, probe the ground as we go and advance carefully.
え～、じゃあじゃあ、<br>この先はずっと雪を踏み抜く可能性があるってことぉ？:
Eh~, so you're saying the whole path ahead
might have these pitfalls everywhere?
一歩間違えたらあんなことになるなんて危険すぎでしょ！: One wrong step and that happens to you—this is way too dangerous!
確かにな。<br>別ルートを探す方が賢明かもしれないな。:
True.
It might be wiser to find another route.
別ルート……。<br>多少戻ってでも、っていうことよね？:
Another route...
That means backtracking a bit, doesn't it?
危険度を考えると、やむを得ないだろう。: Given the danger level, it can't be helped.
一同は来た道を戻り、別ルートを進む。: The group turned back and took a different route.
途中までは良かったが、ここは……厳しいな。: It was fine up to this point, but here...this is a problem.
迂回できない唯一の道に、氷の橋。<br>それも、足１つ乗るかどうかの幅……:
The only path without a detour: an ice bridge.
One that's barely wide enough for a single foot...
絶対足を滑らせて落ちるやつじゃないですかぁ！？: That's absolutely the kind of thing where you slip and fall!?
マリナのその大荷物じゃねぇ～……: With Marina's massive pack on top of that~...
次のルート候補へ行くとしよう。: Let's head for the next route candidate.
吹雪の中を徒労感に包まれつつも迂回し、<br>新たなルートを進む……と。:
Wrapped in a sense of futility, they detoured through the blizzard
and pushed on toward a new route...... only to find.
氷の洞窟のようになっている横穴。<br>その横穴の天井には、つららがびっしりと垂:
A side tunnel that looked like an ice cave.
The ceiling of that tunnel was lined densely with icicles hanging down.
天井に死亡フラグがびっしりじゃないのよ～！<br>ひゃっ！:
The ceiling is absolutely packed with death flags~!
Eek!
地面を踏んだ僅かな振動が伝わって、<br>つららが落ちてきますね～……。:
Even the faintest vibration from stepping on the ground will
send them crashing down~...
ここも無理そうか。<br>残りのルート候補はあと１つだ。そこが使えると良いんだが:
Looks like this one won't work either.
That leaves one more route candidate. Let's hope it works.
更なる徒労感を積み上げ、行軍の果てに辿り着いたのは、<br>絶望的なまでの断崖絶:
After piling on even more frustration, what awaited them
at the end of their march was a hopelessly sheer cliff.
えっと……ここを登るの？: Umm... we're supposed to climb this?
いやいや、こんな氷の壁を登るのは無理無理無理でしょ！: No way no way no way, climbing an ice wall like this is impossible!
途中、角度が９０度越えてるしな……。<br>オーバーハングで脱落者続出になるのが:
The angle goes past 90 degrees partway up too...
It's obvious people would drop off that overhang one after another.
迂回ルートの候補はこれで全部？<br>今まで見た中のどれかを行かなければ先に進め:
Are those all the detour route options?
Does that mean we have to take one of them to get any further?
結局、一番マシなのは、最初のルート、か。<br>あの雪庇を確認しながらゆっくり進:
In the end, the first route is the safest bet.
We'll just have to go slow, checking for cornices along the way.
とりあえず、戻りましょ。<br>このままここにいても、登れるようにはならないわ。:
Anyway, let's head back.
Standing here staring at it won't make it any more climbable.
そうしよう、と頷き、<br>踵を返すと――: He agreed with a nod and turned on his heel——
